content_en:
One of the most theologically distinctive aspects of the Islamic doctrine of jinn is the affirmation that among them are believers (Muslim jinn) and disbelievers (kafir jinn), righteous and wicked, and that their religious diversity parallels in important respects the religious diversity found among human beings. This is established most clearly in Surah Al-Jinn, one of the Quran's most explicit treatments of this world.

The surah opens with the report of jinn who heard the Quran: 'Say: It has been revealed to me that a group of jinn listened, and they said: Indeed, we have heard a wondrous recitation — it guides to rectitude and we have believed in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord.' The jinn's response to the Quran is striking in its comprehensiveness: they affirm its wondrous nature, recognize its guidance, immediately believe, make a declaration of Tawhid, acknowledge their prior errors, and commit to a new path. They then set out to warn their own people — themselves engaging in the practice of dawah.

The same surah describes the diversity among jinn: 'And among us are the righteous and among us are those below that — we were of differing sects.' This mirrors the human experience precisely: there are among jinn those who are upright and those who deviate, just as among humans there are believers of varying levels and disbelievers of varying types. The surah also mentions that after hearing the Quran, the believing jinn recognized the error of their community's association of partners with Allah and the falsehood of their previous beliefs.

The righteous Muslim jinn pray, fast, give zakah (in whatever form is appropriate to their nature), and will enter Paradise. The disbelieving jinn, like human disbelievers, will be held accountable and will enter Hellfire. The Quran explicitly states in Surah Al-Rahman that the message of this surah addresses both jinn and mankind, repeating 'Then which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?' as a refrain directed to both. Both will stand before Allah in accountability.

Al-Ashqar addresses the important question of whether righteous jinn interact with human believers in a positive capacity — providing protection, assistance, or guidance. The consensus of classical scholars is cautious here: while Muslim jinn exist and are righteous, deliberately seeking to establish relationships with jinn — even presumably righteous ones — is not a legitimate Islamic practice. The door to interaction is generally kept closed to protect human beings from the risks of deception and manipulation, given that it is impossible to reliably verify whether a jinn claiming to be Muslim is truthful. The Muslim's protective resource remains the Quran, the authenticated prophetic supplications, and reliance upon Allah alone.
content_id:
Salah satu aspek paling khas secara teologis dari doktrin Islam tentang jin adalah penegasan bahwa di antara mereka ada yang beriman (jin Muslim) dan yang kafir (jin kafir), yang saleh dan yang jahat, dan bahwa keragaman keagamaan mereka dalam hal-hal penting sejajar dengan keragaman keagamaan yang ditemukan di antara manusia. Hal ini ditetapkan paling jelas dalam Surah Al-Jinn, salah satu perlakuan Al-Quran yang paling eksplisit tentang alam ini.

Surah ini dibuka dengan laporan tentang jin yang mendengar Al-Quran: Katakanlah, Telah diwahyukan kepadaku bahwa sekelompok jin telah mendengarkan (Al-Quran), dan mereka berkata: Sesungguhnya kami telah mendengar bacaan yang menakjubkan, yang membimbing kepada kebenaran dan kami telah beriman kepadanya, dan kami tidak akan mempersekutukan seorang pun dengan Tuhan kami. Respons jin terhadap Al-Quran mencolok dalam kelengkapannya: mereka menegaskan sifat menakjubkannya, mengakui petunjuknya, segera beriman, membuat pernyataan Tauhid, mengakui kesalahan-kesalahan mereka sebelumnya, dan berkomitmen pada jalan baru. Mereka kemudian berangkat untuk memperingatkan kaum mereka sendiri, diri mereka sendiri terlibat dalam praktik dakwah.

Surah yang sama menggambarkan keragaman di antara jin: Dan di antara kami ada yang saleh dan di antara kami ada yang tidak demikian, kami adalah golongan-golongan yang berbeda-beda. Ini mencerminkan pengalaman manusia dengan tepat: ada di antara jin mereka yang lurus dan mereka yang menyimpang, sama seperti di antara manusia ada orang-orang beriman dengan berbagai tingkatan dan orang-orang kafir dengan berbagai jenis. Surah ini juga menyebutkan bahwa setelah mendengar Al-Quran, jin-jin yang beriman menyadari kesalahan komunitas mereka dalam mempersekutukan Allah dan kebohongan kepercayaan-kepercayaan mereka sebelumnya.

Jin Muslim yang saleh melaksanakan salat, berpuasa, membayar zakat (dalam bentuk apa pun yang sesuai dengan hakikat mereka), dan akan masuk surga. Jin-jin yang kafir, seperti orang kafir dari manusia, akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban dan akan masuk neraka. Al-Quran secara eksplisit menyatakan dalam Surah Ar-Rahman bahwa pesan surah ini ditujukan kepada jin dan manusia, mengulang Maka nikmat Tuhan kamu yang manakah yang kamu dustakan? sebagai refrain yang ditujukan kepada keduanya. Keduanya akan berdiri di hadapan Allah dalam pertanggungjawaban.

Al-Asyqar membahas pertanyaan penting apakah jin-jin yang saleh berinteraksi dengan orang-orang mukmin secara positif, memberikan perlindungan, bantuan, atau bimbingan. Konsensus ulama-ulama klasik bersifat hati-hati di sini: meskipun jin Muslim ada dan saleh, sengaja mencari untuk menjalin hubungan dengan jin, bahkan yang diduga saleh sekalipun, bukanlah praktik Islam yang sah. Pintu interaksi umumnya dijaga tertutup untuk melindungi manusia dari risiko penipuan dan manipulasi, mengingat tidak mungkin memverifikasi secara andal apakah jin yang mengklaim sebagai Muslim adalah jujur. Sumber perlindungan seorang Muslim tetap pada Al-Quran, doa-doa kenabian yang terotentikasi, dan tawakkal kepada Allah semata.
content_ar: One of the most theologically distinctive aspects of the Islamic doctrine of الجن is the affirmation that among them are المؤمنون (مسلم الجن) and disbelievers (kafir الجن), الصالح and wicked, and that their religious diversity parallels in المهم respects the religious diversity found among human beings. This is established most clearly in السورة Al-الجن, one of the القرآن الكريم's most explicit treatments of this الدنيا. The السورة opens with the report of الجن who heard the القرآن الكريم: 'Say: It has been revealed to me that a group of الجن listened, and قالوا: Indeed, we have heard a wondrous التلاوة — it guides to rectitude and we have believed in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord.' The الجن's response to the القرآن الكريم is striking in its comprehensiveness: they affirm its wondrous nature, recognize its الهداية, immediately believe, make a declaration of Tawhid, acknowledge their prior errors, and commit to a new الطريق. They then set out to warn their own people — themselves engaging in the practice of dawah. The same السورة describes the diversity among الجن: 'And among us are the الصالح and among us are those below that — we were of differing sects.' This mirrors the human experience precisely: there are among الجن those who are upright and those who deviate, just as among humans there are المؤمنون of varying levels and disbelievers of varying types. The السورة أيضاً mentions that بعد hearing the القرآن الكريم, the believing الجن recognized the error of their الجماعة's association of partners with الله and the الباطل of their previous beliefs. The الصالح مسلم الجن pray, fast, give الزكاة (in whatever form is appropriate to their nature), and will enter الجنة. The disbelieving الجن, like human disbelievers, will be held accountable and will enter النار. The القرآن الكريم explicitly states in السورة الرحمن that the message of this السورة addresses both الجن and mankind, repeating 'Then which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?' as a refrain directed to both. Both will stand قبل الله in accountability. Al-Ashqar addresses the المهم question of whether الصالح الجن interact with human المؤمنون in a positive capacity — providing protection, assistance, or الهداية. The consensus of الكلاسيكي العلماء is cautious here: بينما مسلم الجن exist and are الصالح, deliberately seeking to establish relationships with الجن — even presumably الصالح ones — is not a legitimate Islamic practice. The door to interaction is generally kept closed to protect human beings from the risks of deception and manipulation, given that it is impossible to reliably verify whether a الجن claiming to be مسلم is truthful. The مسلم's protective resource remains the القرآن الكريم, the authenticated prophetic supplications, and reliance upon الله alone.
